Mini-Campaign: Therapy as a Culturally Affirming Space

📷 Instagram Square Post

Lead With Clarity

A man gazes over a city skyline at sunset. Teal CTA: "Start Your Healing Today." Targets Black men who associate strength with action — reframing healing as leadership.

📱 Instagram Story

Therapy Shouldn't Be a Lecture

Split-screen: cold waiting room vs. warm teletherapy at home. Addresses the cultural frustration of having to educate a non-Black therapist. Stop educating. Start healing.

Canva Pomelli AI Cultural Pain Point
🖼 Flyer / Poster

Tips for Maintaining Good Mental Health

Branded RHIO Wellness four-quadrant poster: self-care, social connections, stress management, and seeking support. Clean warm-green palette — educational and shareable.

📷 Square Post

Strength Begins With Your Peace

Men's Health Month. Portrait of a man in a warm modern home. Redefines strength for Black men through culturally informed, emotionally resonant language.

Canva Pomelli AI Men's Health Month
📷 Square Post

Breaking Generational Silence

A man relaxes in a library-style room with a tablet. Addresses inherited silence around mental health — inviting a new generation to break cycles through culturally aware therapy.

Canva Pomelli AI Generational Healing
📷 Square Post

Healing Belongs to You

A smiling woman on a couch with a laptop. Positions teletherapy as ownership — healing is not a privilege, it belongs to our communities. Inclusive, affirming language throughout.

Canva Pomelli AI Teletherapy Women's Wellness
Campaign Caption — 100–150 Words
Instagram Caption
✍️ Written Caption

Healing is not weakness — it's the bravest thing you can do. 💚

At RHIO Wellness, we believe therapy should feel like home — a space where you don't have to explain your culture, translate your pain, or start with every session. Our therapists understand where you come from, and that changes everything.

Whether you're navigating stress, breaking generational cycles, or simply ready to invest in yourself — we're here, and we see you.

🌿 Start your healing journey today. Link in bio.

#HealingBelongsToYou #RHIOWellness #CulturallyAffirming #BlackMentalHealth #TherapyIsCultural #MentalHealthMatters #TeletherapySA

Section 3 — Marketing Strategy & Analytics
How I'd Grow RHIO Wellness
How would you help RHIO Wellness attract more therapy or wellness inquiries?
I would lead with culturally resonant content — short-form video (Reels, TikTok) addressing real conversations in Black communities around mental health stigma. Testimonial-style storytelling, educational carousels, and campaign hooks like "Breaking Generational Silence" build trust before pitching services. Pair this with targeted Facebook/Instagram ads focused on urban South African demographics (25–45, Black professionals), and a consistent CTA directing followers to book a free consultation via link in bio.
What social media platforms would you prioritize for RHIO, and why?
Instagram — primary for visual storytelling and the 25–40 demographic. Reels drive organic reach. Facebook — reaches older adults and community groups in SA; great for event promotions and ad targeting. TikTok — growing fast among younger South Africans; ideal for destigmatising mental health through casual, relatable short video. LinkedIn — positions RHIO as a thought leader and attracts corporate wellness partnerships.
What metrics would you track to know whether the marketing is working?
Reach & impressions, engagement rate, click-through rate to booking page, new inquiry volume week-over-week, follower growth, story views, and video completion rate. For paid ads: cost-per-click and cost-per-lead. I'd also track which content types (educational, aspirational, testimonial) drive the most bookings.
How would you report weekly marketing progress to the team?
A one-page weekly snapshot: top 3 performing posts, platform-by-platform metrics, comparison to prior week, what's working and what needs adjustment, and the upcoming content plan. Shared via a Google Doc or Notion page for live team access, with urgent wins or concerns flagged in WhatsApp or Slack.
Section 4 — Mental Health Marketing Approach
Marketing Therapy With Dignity and Cultural Depth
💬

Reducing Stigma Through Language

Avoid clinical or medicalised language. Instead of "mental illness treatment," say "support for your healing journey." Use real cultural reference points — church, family expectations, generational pressure — to show RHIO understands the community, not just the condition.

🚫

Language & Images to Avoid

No images of crying or distressed people. No generic non-African stock photos. No clinical white settings. Avoid language like "disorder," "broken," or "fix yourself." Never position therapy as a last resort — healing should feel proactive and empowering.

🌿

Making RHIO Feel Professional, Warm & Trustworthy

Consistent visual identity — warm greens, teal, earth tones. Cozy home setups, natural light. Feature Black therapists and clients. Testimonials and community voices. A tone that is confident but never condescending — like a trusted friend who also happens to be a professional.

Campaign Brief
Target Audience, Design Choices & Asset Breakdown
ElementDetail
Primary AudienceBlack South Africans aged 25–45, urban professionals, men and women navigating stress, family pressure, and generational trauma. Culturally proud, digitally active, underserved by mainstream therapy.
Secondary AudienceYoung adults (18–25) open to normalising mental health conversations; family members seeking resources for loved ones.
Design ChoicesWarm greens and teal for trust and wellness. Bold readable typography. Real-life home and office settings. Black faces front and centre — as the subject of the story, not as tokens.
Tools UsedCanva (graphics, posters, social posts) · CapCut (video/reel concepts) · Pomelli AI (image generation for campaign visuals) · Lovable (website design) · Google Sites (portfolio site)
Call to Action"Start Your Healing Today" / "Book a Free Consultation" — low-friction, empowering, not clinical. Link in bio to RHIO booking page.
CreatedAll 6 campaign social post visuals, all copy and messaging, campaign concept and strategy, caption, video reel concepts (2 videos), and this portfolio presentation.
AI Image ToolPomelli AI was used to generate the campaign photography — all culturally intentional, featuring Black subjects in affirming, real-life settings. Compositions and creative direction by Delmaine Hoggins.
Templates UsedCanva templates for the RHIO Wellness mental health tips infographic poster. All other layouts and designs are original.
